mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in enterprise environments. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to enhance integration and usability. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the powerful features and enterprise readiness it offers.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid clusters.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

```

> This command downloads and extracts the Tanzu CLI to /usr/local/bin.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> This command initializes the Tanzu CLI and prepares it for use.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> This command sets the kubeconfig context to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the cluster nodes are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> This will remove the cluster and all associated resources, leading to data loss.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm -rf /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> This command deletes the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the Tanzu CLI, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before proceeding with the upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified Kubernetes version.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node count exceeds available resources.

**Fix:**
Reduce the node count or increase available resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl or add it to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the cluster is not set.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster is still initializing or has failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'tanzu cluster get my-cluster' and troubleshoot as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.29, improved CLI performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure your clusters are upgraded to at least Kubernetes v1.27 before upgrading to Tanzu 2.5.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
